creator: louie@TransSys.COM
text:
If mysqld is started as user 'mysql', it doesn't have permissions to
create a socket in /var/run.  According to /etc/mtree/BSD.var.dist,
/var/run has mode 0755 and is owned by root.

Fix: 

I dunno.  Short-term hack is to chmod 1777 /var/run, but this doesn't
seem like the right answer.  Previously, socket was /tmp/mysql.sock
which seemed to work.  Should safe_mysqld create socket before changing
effective user to mysql (or whatever was named?)
How-To-Repeat: 
Install new version of port, run standard ${DESTDIR}/etc/mysql-server.sh
script to run server as mysql user; see hostname.err file in /var/db/mysql
